summary: Matched a broad batch of previously stubbed duplicate-like functions across fighters, items, menus, and one stage file. The author described the tactic as continuing the duplicate-matching pass from #2102: start with an exact duplicate definition and gradually relax it; in this PR, duplicate bodies were allowed to call different exact functions. The decomp.dev report recorded 72 new matches, +7196 matched code bytes, and +8 matched data bytes, with large gains in Crazy Hand and Kirby initialization/special-move code plus many small item state transition functions.

key_files.why: Largest source change; replaced many /// stubs with matched Crazy Hand init/transition/grab helpers such as ftCh_Init_80157DF8, ftCh_Init_80159098, ftCh_Init_801592D4, fn_80159908, ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B174, ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B778, and ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015B850.
Adjusted ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs field types/layout, including splitting Vec2 x50 into float x50 plus s32 x54/x58 and changing xEC from s32 to float, enabling matched Crazy Hand code.

Matched Kirby special/capture helpers including ftKb_SpecialN_800F6070, fn_800F6280, fn_800F6FC4, ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F9614, ftKb_SpecialNLg_800F9684, ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105B2C, ftKb_SpecialNZd_80105BA8, and fn_8010A930.
Matched common fighter helpers fn_800D8E30 and ftCo_800DB464; the latter updates captured fighter position from a bone joint and facing/scale offsets.
Changed Item::xDBC_itcmd_var4 from a raw u32 to a union of flag32 xDBC_itcmd_var4 and u32 xDBC_itcmd_var4_word, allowing both bitfield and raw-word access.
Added/refined item attribute and item-var layouts, including itLipstickAttributes x0 as s32, itLikelike_ItemVars x4C, itWhiteBea_ItemVars fields, and itWhiteBeaAttributes.
Expanded LocalFighterData layout and matched simple stat getter functions mnCount_802502CC, fn_80250338, fn_8025069C, fn_802506C0, fn_802506E4, and fn_80250708.
Adjusted grKongo_801D52FC to keep the Ground_801C2BA4 return gobj and pass it to ftCo_800C0764, replacing a commented-out call.

what_changed: Replaced many /// # stubs with C implementations for duplicate-like functions across 44 files, totaling 724 added and 103 deleted lines.

Crazy Hand gained numerous matched motion-state entry helpers that follow a repeated pattern: GET_FIGHTER, optional ftCrazyHand_DatAttrs access, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and setup of mv.ch fields, cmd_vars, callbacks, position, velocity, or grab/capture flags.
Kirby gained matched special-move/copy-ability helpers, including Hammer-related setup, Lickitung/Zelda special-N setup, and Kirby-Yoshi capture setup that uses ftCo_800DB368 and ftCo_800DB464.
Common fighter code matched fn_800D8E30 with PAD_STACK plus fall transition calls, and ftCo_800DB464 with bone-position calculation through lb_8000B1CC and ftParts_GetBoneIndex.
Many item files gained small state transition functions that reset velocity, switch item motion with Item_80268E5C, call shared item helpers, set hitlag callbacks, or initialize item variables.
Item command flag handling was cleaned up by introducing a union for xDBC_itcmd_var4 and replacing casts/commented bit twiddling with item->xDBC_itcmd_var4.flags.x0 or xDBC_itcmd_var4_word.
Several headers replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes using Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, s32, or void*.
Menu code matched simple persistent-fighter-stat getters in mncount and a name-entry cleanup helper fn_8023D0F8 that frees text/SisLib state.
decomp.dev reported matched code increasing to 50.28%, up 0.19%, with 72 new matches and 8 improvements in unmatched functions/data sections.
smart_moves: The author explicitly used a controlled duplicate-discovery strategy: start with strict identical bodies and progressively relax the duplicate definition; this PR relaxed exact callee identity while still requiring otherwise similar structure.
Many matches were unlocked by recognizing repeated state-entry templates rather than solving each function from scratch, especially Fighter_ChangeMotionState plus ftAnim_8006EBA4 setup functions.
Header and struct repairs were made in the same PR as function matches, preventing matched implementations from relying on casts or incorrect UNK prototypes.
The Item xDBC union preserved raw u32 zeroing where needed while allowing typed flag access elsewhere, reducing fragile manual bit manipulation.
Local inline/helper functions were introduced where they improved emitted code shape, such as ftCh_FingerGun2_Anim_inline/inline2 and ftKb_SpecialNZd_Helper.
PAD_STACK was used in several small functions where stack shape mattered, including fighter and item transition helpers.
decomp_lessons: For duplicate-matching passes, relaxing callee identity can reveal many simple functions that are structurally identical except for which helper they call.
Small state transition functions often match by preserving the exact order of: fetch object data, reset vars/velocity/flags, call motion-state change, then run animation setup.
Do not leave function pointers or callbacks as generic stubs when the duplicate body reveals their exact type; add static declarations or header prototypes with the correct GObj type.
Struct field type correctness can be decisive: using float vs s32, splitting vector-like guesses into scalar fields, or adding missing padding can unlock codegen.
When a field is accessed both as flags and as a whole word, a union can match code more cleanly than casts or duplicated fields.

For item functions, common duplicate patterns include it_8026B390 plus itResetVelocity plus Item_80268E5C, or spawn/setup functions that initialize item vars and special attributes before entering the first item state.
For fighter capture/carry logic, exact state flags and callback assignments such as accessory1_cb/accessory4_cb are part of the match, not just the motion-state ID.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based names for unknown or not-yet-semantically-named functions, e.g. fn_80159908, ftCh_Init_80159098, it_802E4190, and fn_80250338.
Used subsystem prefixes consistently: ftCh_ for Crazy Hand, ftKb_ for Kirby, ftCo_ for common fighter/capture code, it_ for items, mnCount_ and mnNameNew_ for menu code.
Headers include address comments beside prototypes, preserving the project's address-indexed navigation convention.
Unknown struct members remain offset-style names such as x54, x58, xEC, xDD4_itemVar.whitebea.x40, and mv.ch.unk0.x38 when semantics are not proven.
Object pointer names generally follow project style: gobj for object handles, fp for Fighter*, ip for Item*, da or attrs for data attributes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with sizes such as 8, 12, 20, and 4 * 14 to reproduce stack allocation/codegen in otherwise simple functions.
Introduced static inline helpers and wrapper helpers to influence call/inlining structure where duplicate matching required it.
Matched functions with repeated zeroing idioms such as fp->self_vel.x = fp->self_vel.y = fp->self_vel.z = 0 and position vector z reset to 0.0f.
Replaced raw bit shifts/casts with flag32 bitfield access where the emitted code matched, while retaining xDBC_itcmd_var4_word for whole-word clearing.
Corrected function prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures so callers could compile to the expected ABI and call sequence.
Adjusted local struct padding and scalar field widths in mncount to access persistent fighter data at exact offsets.

review_feedback.feedback: The author stated the approach was the same as #2102 and explained the duplicate search methodology: begin with exact duplicates except jump addresses, then allow called functions to differ; future steps may allow constants to differ.
Reported 72 new matches, +7196 matched code bytes, +8 matched data bytes, and matched code rising to 50.28% (+0.19%).
No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that relaxed duplicate matching to allow differing constants, as forecast in this PR body.
Search for functions still using /// # stubs near the newly matched Crazy Hand and Kirby helpers to continue duplicate-template matching.
Audit other item command variable fields for opportunities to replace raw u32/casts with unions or flag32 bitfields.
Look for additional item state transition functions that follow it_8026B390 + itResetVelocity + Item_80268E5C patterns.
Compare PR #2102 with PR #2110 to document the duplicate matching progression and reusable heuristics.
